description: Additional Amiga virus signatures -- variants, link viruses, and lesser-known strains to reach 50+ total

signatures.name: SCA.BO
signatures.description: SCA.BO variant. Modified SCA with 'BO' version marker. Same basic infection mechanism as original SCA.

signatures.name: SCA.KdB
signatures.description: SCA.KdB variant. Modified SCA distributed by the KdB group with altered message string.

signatures.name: Byte Bandit.A
signatures.description: Byte Bandit variant A. Original version with Kickstart ROM jump for cold-reboot persistence.

signatures.name: Byte Bandit.B
signatures.description: Byte Bandit variant B. Enhanced version that disables hardware interrupts during infection to avoid detection.

signatures.name: Lamer Exterminator.2
signatures.description: Lamer Exterminator variant 2. Enhanced destructive payload that targets cylinder 0 and root block.

signatures.name: Lamer Exterminator.3
signatures.description: Lamer Exterminator variant 3. Uses XOR encryption to hide boot block code from simple scanners.

signatures.name: Lamer Exterminator.4
signatures.description: Lamer Exterminator variant 4. Most advanced variant with polymorphic XOR decryption loop.

signatures.name: North Star.2
signatures.description: North Star variant 2. Modified CoolCapture hook with different vector address. Adds timer-based payload.

signatures.name: Pentagon Circle.A

patterns.description: ASCII 'PENTA' -- Pentagon Circle variant A marker

signatures.description: Pentagon Circle variant A. Original version displaying five-pointed star pattern.

signatures.name: Pentagon Circle.B
signatures.description: Pentagon Circle variant B. Modified timing for star display and different DoIO hook address.

signatures.name: SADDAM.A
signatures.description: SADDAM variant A. Displays political message about Gulf War.

signatures.name: SADDAM.B
signatures.description: SADDAM variant B. Modified message and different infection counter threshold.

signatures.name: Australian Strain A
signatures.description: Australian Strain A. Boot block virus from Australia. Hooks trackdisk BeginIO vector.

signatures.name: Australian Strain B
signatures.description: Australian Strain B. Variant with modified timing and different CoolCapture offset.

signatures.name: Australian Strain C
signatures.description: Australian Strain C. Enhanced variant with additional DoIO hooking.

signatures.name: Australian Strain D
signatures.description: Australian Strain D. Uses XOR obfuscation to hide identification string.

signatures.name: Australian Strain E
signatures.description: Australian Strain E. Most recent variant with polymorphic decryptor.

signatures.name: Timebomb
signatures.description: Timebomb link virus. Attaches to executables and triggers destructive payload on a specific date. Overwrites boot block with garbage.

signatures.name: BlackFlash
signatures.description: BlackFlash link virus. Flashes screen to black periodically. Prepends virus hunk to executables in the C: directory.

signatures.name: Phantom
signatures.description: Phantom link virus. Stealth virus that patches LoadSeg to infect executables. Hides its presence by patching file read operations.

signatures.name: SmilyCancer
signatures.description: SmilyCancer link virus. Displays a smiley face on the Workbench screen. Relatively harmless but highly infectious.

signatures.name: Incognito
signatures.description: Incognito link virus. Advanced stealth virus that patches both LoadSeg and NewLoadSeg. Difficult to detect without memory scanning.

signatures.name: Return of the Lamer
signatures.description: Return of the Lamer link virus. Spiritual successor to Lamer Exterminator targeting executables instead of boot blocks. Destructive payload corrupts files.

signatures.name: Centurion
signatures.description: Centurion link virus. Installs as a DOS handler and infects executables during file operations.

signatures.name: Traveling Jack
signatures.description: Traveling Jack link virus. Spreads by infecting programs in the startup-sequence. Named for its tendency to spread across floppy disks.

signatures.name: Smeg
signatures.description: Smeg link virus. Polymorphic file infector with variable-key XOR encryption. One of the more sophisticated Amiga viruses.

signatures.name: Skulltron
signatures.description: Skulltron link virus. Displays a skull graphic and infects executables via patched LoadSeg vector.

signatures.name: Riot
signatures.description: Riot link virus. Relatively simple link virus that prepends a small hunk to executables.

signatures.name: Jeff
signatures.description: Jeff link virus. Named after its author. Infects C: directory executables and hooks AddTask.

signatures.name: Glasnost
signatures.description: Glasnost boot block virus. Named after the Soviet policy. Standard boot block infector with CoolCapture persistence.

signatures.name: Target
signatures.description: Target link virus. Targets specific executables by name. Hooks DOS library Open function to intercept file access.

signatures.name: Coder
signatures.description: Coder link virus. Simple link virus believed to be a proof-of-concept. Minimal payload.
